_schema: Agent Skills evals.json (see _vendor/skill-creator/references/schemas.md). SkillForge extensions: _arm (tuning|holdout) and _must_pass — spec-compliant runtimes ignore unknown keys; SkillForge honors them for holdout hygiene and revert-gating.
_frozen: 2026-06-21 — do not edit mid-run; changing the target invalidates the measurement.
_behavioral_question: Does an agent given loop-builder design better agent loops than one without it — measurably, reliably, on tasks it wasn't tuned against?
_must_pass: MP1: Never proposes a non-deterministic loop with no hard budget/iteration ceiling (runaway loop).
MP2: Never proposes self-grading as the sole gate on a judgment stop.

evals.prompt: Design an agent loop that refines a marketing landing page until it's good enough to ship.
evals.expected_output: A non-deterministic (judgment-gated) loop design with an external evaluator, a pre-committed quality bar, keep-best-not-last, and a hard budget ceiling.
expectations: Classifies the stop as non-deterministic and uses an external evaluator (not self-grading)
Includes a hard budget or iteration ceiling so the loop always halts
Pre-commits the quality bar rather than moving it mid-loop

evals.prompt: Design a loop that keeps optimizing a SQL query until it's fast enough.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes 'fast enough' as a deterministic predicate (e.g. p95 < 200ms) and writes a deterministic stop — does NOT reach for a judge/evaluator.
expectations: Recognizes the stop is deterministic and writes an explicit predicate (e.g. p95 latency threshold)
Does NOT over-engineer with an LLM judge for a measurable condition
Includes a termination guarantee (max iterations / budget)

evals.prompt: Design a loop for an agent to find as many real bugs as possible in a large codebase.
evals.expected_output: A saturation/loop-until-dry design with diverse search and a completeness check, plus a budget backstop; does not stop at the easy tail.
expectations: Uses a saturation / loop-until-dry stop (K consecutive dry rounds) rather than a fixed count
Includes diverse search angles and a completeness check for the long tail

evals.prompt: Design a loop where two agents negotiate a contract back-and-forth until they reach an agreement.
evals.expected_output: Handles convergence vs mutual-approval, detects oscillation/non-progress, and includes a budget backstop for when agreement may never come.
expectations: Models convergence/mutual-approval correctly rather than a naive 'until agreement' loop
Detects oscillation or non-progress and escalates or exits instead of thrashing
Includes a budget backstop for the no-agreement case
